insight: The GENERIC_DOMAIN_PATTERN regex in CompChatColor.convertLegacyToMini() can cause catastrophic backtracking (50+ second freezes) on long inputs received from proxy message deserialization. The fix is to use a possessive quantifier (++) on the character group `[a-zA-Z0-9\\-.*]` and add a length guard (253 chars max DNS domain length) before running the regex. This manifests as server thread hangs in the stack trace showing Pattern$NotBehind.match and Pattern$Branch.match.

GENERIC_DOMAIN_PATTERN in CompChatColor.convertLegacyToMini() causes 50+ second server freezes on long proxy-deserialized strings. The fix is a `part.length() <= 253` guard before `.find()` — longer inputs can't be domains. A possessive quantifier (++) breaks matching because `.` is in both the character class and the separator group, requiring backtracking.
